items.title: Watch Yaxel Lendeborg drop 21 points, grab 10 rebounds to lead Warriors to Summer League title
items.summary: It was clear why Golden State and Memphis were facing off in Las Vegas for the Summer League title Sunday night. Usually, when a high draft pick has a few good games at Summer League, the team pulls him for the rest of the event to avoid potential injury.But Memphis took the court with two likely starters from the team that will take the court in October: No. 3 pick Cameron Boozer and second-year forward Cedric Coward. Golden State took the court with No. 11 pick Yaxel Lendeborg — and he was the best player on that court, scoring 21 points with 10 rebounds, leading the summer Warriors to the championship.Yaxel Lendeborg, the 11th overall pick, wins the 2026 NBA Summer League Championship Game MVP! 21 PTS 10 REB 2 STL WARRIORS W pic.twitter.com/bSa4XbBBi2— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026Golden State came from 10 down at the start of the fourth quarter to get the 94-40 victory. His performance…

items.title: Warriors win Summer League behind Yaxel Lendeborg, Deivon Smith
items.summary: LAS VEGAS, NEVADA - JULY 19: Yaxel Lendeborg #1 of the Golden State Warriors holds up the 026 NBA Las Vegas Summer League MVP trophy and championship ring after his team's victory over the Memphis Grizzlies in the 2026 NBA Summer League championship game at the Thomas & Mack Center on July 19, 2026 in Las Vegas, Nevada. The Warriors defeated the Grizzlies 94-90. NOTE TO USER: User expressly acknowledges and agrees that, by downloading and or using this photograph, User is consenting to the terms and conditions of the Getty Images License Agreement. (Photo by Ian Maule/Getty Images) | Getty Images All Yaxel Lendeborg does is win championships.Three months after his Michigan Wolverines won the NCAA Tournament, Lendeborg led the Golden State Warriors to their first NBA Summer League championship since 2013 with a 94-90 win over the Memphis Grizzlies. He finished with 21 points and…

items.title: 2026 Summer League: Golden State Warriors hold off Grizzlies for title
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors earned the 2026 NBA Summer League championship on Sunday, July 19.Yaxel Lendeborg led the Warriors in the 94-90 victory over the Memphis Grizzlies. He scored 21 and had 10 rebounds for the Warriors in 32 minutes of play.Lendeborg, the Warriors’ 11th overall pick in the 2026 draft, hit a jumper that helped provide a 91-87 lead for the Warriors with 1:30 left in the fourth quarter and put the game out of reach for the Grizzlies.Lendeborg was named the Summer League championship game MVP.Deivon Smith also added 21 points and nine rebounds in 23 minutes of play for Golden State. He shot 9-for-14 from the field.Cameron Boozer, the No. 3 overall pick in 2026, served as one of the leading scorers for the Grizzlies. He nearly produced a double-double with 19 points and eight rebounds.Cedric Coward scored 19 points and collected six rebounds in 27 minutes of…

items.title: Dillon Brooks calls out Luka Doncic, Steph Curry and LeBron James
items.summary: Dillon Brooks didn’t hesitate to call out Luka Doncic, LeBron James and Steph Curry.Brooks, who plays for the Suns, was asked who are the top-five complainers in basketball and listed Doncic, James and Curry on that list as well as Jaren Jackson and Giannis Antetokounmpo.Suns star Dillon Brooks said LeBron James (above) is one of the NBA’s biggest whiners. Getty Images“First one that comes to mind for me? Obviously, Luka. Luka’s obviously a big-ass complainer,” Brooks said. “Curry is one, I actually gave him a shot. I gave him a shot in his chest on a contest.”Doncic has admitted that he believes Brooks is the NBA’s biggest trash talker and said he loves to play against him.Doncic and Brooks used to tangle when Doncic played for the Mavericks and Brooks suited up for the Rockets.In his career, Doncic has thrived against him, averaging 29.4 points, 7.6 rebounds and 7.1 assists per…

items.title: Winners, losers from Lu Dort trade to Atlanta in three-team deal
items.summary: It's not unexpected that Lu Dort got traded this offseason.Oklahoma City felt the pressure of the second apron closing in as the max extensions for Chet Holmgren and Jalen Williams kicked in for next season, and some depth was going to be sacrificed. Isaiah Joe and Aaron Wiggins were already gone, but Dort is making $18.2 million next season and is up for an extension the Thunder couldn't swing, so the sides were always likely to part.Atlanta stepped up, Dallas jumped in, and we have a three-team trade. Who are the winners and losers? Let's break it all down, starting with a reminder of the trade itself:Oklahoma City receives: Three future second-round picksAtlanta receives: Luguentz Dort, Ryan NembhardDallas receives: Zaccharie RisacherWinner: Atlanta HawksThis just adds to what has been a strong offseason for the Hawks — they got the best player in this trade.Atlanta is…

items.title: Yaxel Lendeborg outduels Cameron Boozer to lead Warriors past Grizzlies for NBA Summer League championship
items.summary: The Golden State Warriors selected Yaxel Lendeborg with the No. 11 pick in the NBA Draft hoping that the 23-year-old rookie with an NCAA title from Michigan is ready to contribute right away.He looked the part Sunday night while leading the Warriors to a 94-90 win over the Grizzles in the NBA Summer League championship game in Las Vegas that featured a high-profile matchup against against Memphis rookie Cameron Boozer.The game was tied at 47 at halftime. Boozer exploded for 10 of his 19 points in the third quarter, including a 3-pointer in the final seconds to extend a Grizzlies lead to 76-66.Cameron Boozer sinks the 3 at the end of Q3 🎯The 3rd overall pick has a game-high 17 PTS!Memphis leads by 10 heading into Q4 of the NBA Summer League Championship. pic.twitter.com/8uJhg3OQCY— NBA (@NBA) July 20, 2026But the fourth quarter belonged to Lendeborg, who scored seven straight Warriors…
